message:
feat(frankenpandas): expand prelude with path-based IO + add Path import to Recipes (br-m3zib / fd90.125)

fd90.125: follow-on to fd90.121. The Financial Data Pipeline recipe at
README lines 1303-1329 imports 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*;' then
calls write_jsonl(&vwap, Path::new(...))? — but neither write_jsonl
nor std::path::Path were available.

A user copy-pasting hits two errors:
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'write_jsonl' in this scope
  error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of undeclared type 'Path'

Fix:
1. Expand prelude with path-based IO readers/writers (already pub-used
   at top-level, just absent from prelude):
     readers: read_csv, read_feather, read_json, read_jsonl, read_parquet
     writers: write_csv, write_feather, write_json, write_jsonl, write_parquet
   Symmetric with the bytes/string variants added in fd90.121.

2. Add 'use std::path::Path;' to the Recipes example (Path stays
   outside the frankenpandas surface — that's std lib).

Verified:
- cargo build -p frankenpandas — compiles
- cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib — all 5 prelude tests pass
  (prelude_smoke_test, prelude_concat_works, prelude_query_works,
   prelude_io_roundtrip, prelude_to_datetime_works)

Pure prelude expansion — no behavior change. Items were already pub-used
at the top-level frankenpandas crate (lib.rs:51-147).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -199,23 +199,33 @@ pub mod prelude {
         concat_dataframes,
         concat_series,
         merge_ordered,
-        // IO — readers
+        // IO — readers (in-memory + path)
+        read_csv,
         read_csv_str,
+        read_feather,
         read_feather_bytes,
+        read_json,
         read_json_str,
+        read_jsonl,
         read_jsonl_str,
+        read_parquet,
         read_parquet_bytes,
         read_sql,
         read_sql_table,
         // IO — datetime helpers
         to_datetime,
         to_numeric,
         to_timedelta,
-        // IO — writers (string + bytes + sql, matching README documented surface)
+        // IO — writers (in-memory + path + sql, matching README documented surface)
+        write_csv,
         write_csv_string,
+        write_feather,
         write_feather_bytes,
+        write_json,
         write_json_string,
+        write_jsonl,
         write_jsonl_string,
+        write_parquet,
         write_parquet_bytes,
         write_sql,
     };
